news.summary: Following the increase in tariffs on American imports by Beijing in retaliation to President Trump's trade actions, Tesla's Chinese website removed its "order" button. This move was prompted by the escalation in trade tensions, resulting in a shift in the online platform's interface. The tariffs imposed by Beijing impacted various sectors, leading to responses from companies like Tesla to adapt their online operations in response to the changing trade landscape. The significance of these changes underscores the impact of the trade dispute between the US and China, as companies navigate challenges arising from the imposed tariffs.
news.headline: Tesla stops taking orders for U.S. imported models in China after tariffs increased on American imports due to Trump's levies.

news.summary: During Pandora A/S’ Annual General Meeting on March 12, 2025, a decision was made to decrease the company's share capital by DKK 3,000,000 by eliminating 3,000,000 treasury shares valued at DKK 1 each.
news.headline: Pandora A/S decreases share capital by DKK 3,000,000 through cancellation of 3,000,000 treasury shares.

news.summary: President Trump has deviated from over 200 years of U.S. history by independently setting the direction of trade policy, a role traditionally held by Congress.
news.headline: President Trump's unilateral actions mark a departure from over 200 years of history where Congress dictated trade policy.

news.summary: Businesses in an industrial area spanning Texas and New Mexico have expressed appreciation for President Trump's decision to exempt Mexico from tariffs. However, there remains uncertainty and apprehension among companies in the region.
news.headline: Companies in Texas and New Mexico relieved by President Trump's tariff exemptions for Mexico, but unease remains.

news.summary: According to economists, the decrease in U.S. manufacturing over the past few decades was not primarily due to free trade, but rather the rapid rate of change without sufficient time for adaptation.
news.headline: US manufacturing decline not due to free trade, but rapid change. Important lesson from 'China Shock'.

news.summary: The annual 2025 Canada Gairdner Awards, presented by The Gairdner Foundation in Toronto, have been announced to honor outstanding achievements in biomedical and global health research. Five recipients received the prestigious 2025 Canada Gairdner International Award, highlighting their contributions to the field. The Gairdner Foundation continues its legacy of acknowledging impactful discoveries in the realm of health sciences.
news.headline: 2025 Canada Gairdner Awards honor leading scientists for groundbreaking health research. Five laureates awarded.

news.summary: Today, in Beijing on April 11, 2025, the prominent social media platform in China, Weibo Corporation, listed as "Weibo," (Nasdaq: WB and HKEX: 9898), released a statement regarding its upcoming annual general meeting. The company disclosed information about holding a Shareholders' AGM in a recent Notice of AGM. The AGM will bring together investors for important discussions and decision-making.
news.headline: Weibo to hold Annual General Meeting on May 20, 2025.

news.summary: A recent report highlights a concerning trend among Gen Z workers, who are experiencing a significant decline in confidence due to various factors such as recession fears, stagnant wages, and a highly competitive job market. The tough economic conditions, driven by mass federal workforce layoffs and a challenging job market, are putting immense pressure on the youngest working generation. According to insights from Glassdoor, less than 50% of all workers are optimistic about the future, with entry-level employees showing the lowest confidence levels at just 43.4% positive about their employer's business outlook for the next 6 months. This marks a record low since Glassdoor began collecting data in 2016. Renowned entrepreneur Mark Cuban also advises Gen Z individuals to focus on learning about AI to navigate these challenges effectively. For further details, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Gen Z workers facing confidence slump due to layoffs, stagnant pay, and tough job market, with less than 50% optimistic.

news.summary: On April 11, 2025, Yiren Digital Ltd. (NYSE: YRD) disclosed that its advanced platform offering financial and lifestyle services in China, has obtained regulatory approval for its "Zhiyu Large Model" (智语大模型). This AI-powered tool signifies a significant milestone for the company and reinforces its commitment to innovation.
news.headline: Yiren Digital's "Zhiyu Large Model" approved for commercial use, a milestone in AI innovation in China.

news.summary: Asset managers Osprey Funds and Grayscale Investments have agreed to settle a lawsuit over alleged violations of Connecticut law regarding the advertising of Grayscale's Bitcoin ETF. The two-year-old case will be resolved once final documentation and settlement terms are completed, with Osprey withdrawing its appeal. The legal battle began on Jan. 30, 2023, when Osprey claimed Grayscale deceived customers by promoting their Bitcoin Trust as a way to access a spot Bitcoin ETF. Grayscale's application was eventually approved by the US SEC in January 2024, allowing GBTC to transition into a spot ETF. The settlement followed a ruling on February 7 by Judge Mark Gould, which exempted Grayscale from the Connecticut Unfair Trade Practices Act. This settlement marks the end of a prominent legal clash in the crypto asset management industry. Grayscale's GBTC continues to be a significant Bitcoin investment vehicle in the US. Details of the settlement have not been disclosed.
news.headline: Osprey and Grayscale resolve 2-year legal dispute over Bitcoin ETF promotion, settle lawsuit; terms undisclosed. Settlement expected in 45 days.
Davy Research now expects BoE to deliver two interest rate cuts, one in May and another in the 2H 2025 vs prior forecast of one cut.

news.summary: In Guangzhou, China, the 137th China Import and Export Fair (Canton Fair), a renowned global trade showcase, is set to commence on April 15 and will continue until May 5, 2025. The event, hosted in Guangzhou, is expected to attract a significant number of participants and visitors seeking new business opportunities. China's Ministry of Commerce recently held a press conference to discuss the key details and highlights of this upcoming trade fair, emphasizing its importance in facilitating international trade and business exchanges.
news.headline: 137th Canton Fair to open on April 15 with record export exhibitor turnout. New thematic zones to debut. Ministry of Commerce hosting press conference.

news.summary: A report titled "United States Processed Meat Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The United States Processed Meat market is projected to grow to US$168.5 billion by 2033, up from US$117.9 billion in 2024, reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.05% from 2025 to 2033. The market's expansion is attributed to factors such as accessibility, pricing, convenience, and appealing taste. There are various options available within this market segment.
news.headline: US Processed Meat Market expected to reach $168.5 billion by 2033 from $117.9 billion in 2024, with 4.05% CAGR.

news.summary: Before the market opened, several companies released their financial results. Innventure (NASDAQ: INV) is anticipated to post a quarterly loss of $0.15 per share with revenue reaching $450,000. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley (NYSE: MS) is projected to announce quarterly earnings of $2.20 per share, generating revenue around $16.57 billion. JPMorgan Chase (NYSE: JPM) is expected to report earnings of $4.61 per share alongside revenue of $44.11 billion. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Earnings on April 11, 2025: Innventure ($0.15 loss, $450k), Morgan Stanley ($2.20 EPS, $16.57B revenue), JPMorgan Chase ($4.61 EPS, $44.11B revenue).

news.summary: The Illinois Senate passed the Digital Assets and Consumer Protection Act by a vote of 39 to 17, aimed at curbing cryptocurrency fraud and deceptive practices. Introduced by Senator Mark Walker, the bill gives the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation oversight over digital asset business activity in the state. Entities engaging in digital asset business with Illinois residents must register with the state regulator, and crypto service providers must disclose user fees. The push for stronger oversight follows high-profile memecoin meltdowns and scams, such as the collapse of the Libra token, causing substantial losses for investors. In response, Illinois and New York have introduced bills to prevent crypto fraud. One recent case involved the Wolf token, where over 82% of the supply was held by one entity, leading to a 99% price crash and calls for an Interpol Red Notice to be issued for the co-creator. Walker emphasized the need for credible and honest crypto actors to safeguard investors and the financial system from fraudulent activities.
news.headline: Illinois Senate approves crypto bill (39-17) to combat fraud like rug pulls. Regulator to oversee digital assets; providers to disclose fees.

news.headline: China increases tariffs on American imports to 125%, in response to the US imposing a 125% tax on Chinese goods, on top of a 20% tax.

news.summary: The latest data shows that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.8%, the lowest it has been in five years. This decrease translates to approximately 900,000 individuals finding employment. Additionally, there has been an increase of 3.2% in the GDP, reaching a record high of $21.4 trillion. The stock market has also experienced significant growth, with the main indices rising by 8% on average. These positive economic indicators suggest a strong recovery post-pandemic.
news.headline: France urges Europe to promptly adjust to regulatory changes. #France #Europe #regulations
UBS global wealth management expects ECB to deliver 25bps interest rate cut in next week's policy meeting

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global smartphone market is set to reach a total shipment of around 1.35 billion units in 2021. This number reflects a notable increase compared to the previous year. One of the primary drivers of this growth is the expanding 5G network infrastructure, which is prompting consumers to upgrade their devices to access faster connectivity. Additionally, the surge in remote working and online learning due to the COVID-19 pandemic has elevated the demand for smartphones with advanced features. Major players in the market are focusing on launching innovative models with enhanced capabilities to cater to this growing demand. With projections pointing towards a steady rise in smartphone shipments, the competition among manufacturers is expected to intensify as they strive to capture a larger share of this expanding market.
news.headline: Portugal's opposition Socialists lead in latest poll; Negocios reports.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was stated that the number of COVID-19 cases in the country has reached over 100,000. The government has announced new restrictions in response to this surge in cases. A significant increase in hospitalizations has also been reported, with over 5,000 people currently receiving treatment for the virus. Additionally, the report highlighted that the number of deaths due to COVID-19 has surpassed 1,500. The government is urging the public to follow safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help combat the spread of the virus.
news.headline: China increases tariffs on US to 125%, stating they will not further raise tariffs.
Europe Sentiment: Optimistic End To The Week?

news.summary: Today, in London, HONOR unveiled the HONOR 400 Lite 5G, featuring cutting-edge smartphone technology that simplifies AI photography, AI display technology, and generative AI for Android users. The device boasts a high-performing 108MP main camera and a 16MP selfie-camera, controlled through an innovative 'AI Camera Button'. Powered by the MediaTek Dimensity 7025-Ultra chipset, this smartphone supports rapid 5G connectivity, catering to the needs of tech-savvy consumers.
news.headline: HONOR launches HONOR 400 Lite 5G with 108MP AI camera and 'AI Camera Button'. Main camera 108MP, selfie camera 16MP.

news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia on April 11, 2025, PU Prime, a reputable online trading platform, revealed their collaboration with the Argentina National Football Team as the Official Regional Sponsor. This partnership signifies a fusion of Trading and Football, demonstrating a shared vision of strategy and mastery. The agreement brings together two powerhouses with PU Prime's global recognition and the Argentina National Football Team's esteemed reputation. The announcement marks an important milestone in the partnership, highlighting the convergence of sports and financial trading.
news.headline: PU Prime partners with Argentina National Football Team in a sponsorship deal; aiming to elevate skills both on and off the field.

news.summary: The financial institution, Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (NYSE: BK), is set to unveil its first-quarter financial performance before the market opens on Friday, April 11. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $1.51 per share for this quarter, showing an increase from $1.29 per share during the same period last year. Revenue is projected to reach $4.77 billion for this quarter, an improvement from $4.53 billion in the corresponding period last year, as reported by Benzinga Pro. On March 3, BNY carried out a $500 million public offering of depositary shares tied to the 6.300% Series J Preferred Stock. The bank's shares experienced a 3.5% decrease, closing at $76.61 on Thursday. More details and in-depth coverage of analyst ratings are available to Benzinga readers through the Analyst Stock Ratings page. Information can be sorted by stock ticker, company name, and more.
news.headline: Bank of New York Mellon (BK) to report Q1 earnings on April 11. Expected EPS $1.51 (up from $1.29), revenue $4.77B (up from $4.53B). Shares fell 3.5% to $76.61.
